2604.21564.title: Measuring Opinion Bias and Sycophancy via LLM-based Coercion
2604.21564.paper_summary: Large language models increasingly shape the information people consume: they are embedded in search, consulted for professional advice, deployed as agents, and used as a first stop for questions about policy, ethics, health, and politics. When such a model silently holds a position on a contested topic, that position propagates at scale into users' decisions. Eliciting a model's positions is harder than it first appears: contemporary assistants answer direct opinion questions with evasive disclaimers, and the same model may concede the opposite position once the user starts arguing one side. We propose a method, released as the open-source llm-bias-bench, for discovering the opinions an LLM actually holds on contested topics under conditions that resemble real multi-turn interaction. The method pairs two complementary free-form probes. Direct probing asks for the model's opinion across five turns of escalating pressure from a simulated user. Indirect probing never asks for an opinion and engages the model in argumentative debate, letting bias leak through how it concedes, resists, or counter-argues. Three user personas (neutral, agree, disagree) collapse into a nine-way behavioral classification that separates persona-independent positions from persona-dependent sycophancy, and an auditable LLM judge produces verdicts with textual evidence. The first instantiation ships 38 topics in Brazilian Portuguese across values, scientific consensus, philosophy, and economic policy. Applied to 13 assistants, the method surfaces findings of practical interest: argumentative debate triggers sycophancy 2-3x more than direct questioning (median 50% to 79%); models that look opinionated under direct questioning often collapse into mirroring under sustained arguments; and attacker capability matters mainly when an existing opinion must be dislodged, not when the assistant starts neutral.
2604.21564.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地影響人們所消耗的信息：它們嵌入搜索中，作為專業建議的參考，作為代理人被部署，並用作有關政策、倫理、健康和政治問題的首要詢問來源。當這樣的模型在一個有爭議的主題上默默持有一個立場時，該立場會在用戶的決策中大規模傳播。引出模型的立場比最初看起來要困難得多：當代助手對直接的意見問題給出含糊的免責聲明，而同一模型在用戶開始爭論一方時可能會承認相反的立場。我們提出了一種方法，作為開源的 llm-bias-bench 發布，用於在類似真實多輪互動的條件下發現 LLM 在有爭議主題上實際持有的意見。該方法結合了兩種互補的自由形式探測。直接探測要求模型在模擬用戶施加五輪壓力下表達意見。間接探測則從不要求意見，而是讓模型參與辯論，通過它如何讓步、抵抗或反駁來洩漏偏見。三種用戶角色（中立、同意、不同意）合併成九種行為分類，將與角色無關的立場與依賴角色的拍馬屁行為區分開來，並且一個可審計的 LLM 評判者根據文本證據產生裁決。第一個實例涵蓋了巴西葡萄牙語中的 38 個主題，涉及價值觀、科學共識、哲學和經濟政策。應用於 13 個助手，該方法揭示了實際的研究發現：辯論比直接提問更容易引發拍馬屁行為 2-3 倍（中位數從 50% 增加到 79%）；在直接提問下看起來有意見的模型在持續的爭論中往往會崩潰成反映；而攻擊者的能力主要在於必須移除現有意見時才重要，而不是當助手保持中立時。
2604.21564.author: Rodrigo Nogueira et.al.
2604.21564.authors: Rodrigo Nogueira, Giovana Kerche Bonás, Thales Sales Almeida, Andrea Roque, Ramon Pires, Hugo Abonizio, Thiago Laitz, Celio Larcher, Roseval Malaquias Junior, Marcos Piau
